figures.caption: Figure 1: An example of multi-hop reasoning over an incomplete knowledge graph. Existing methods (middle) solely rely on explicit edges and fail to perform multi-hop reasoning under graph incompleteness (e.g. missing edges). Our method (bottom) overcomes this limitation by exploiting implicit relations in the graph structure.

Table 1: Performance comparison on four KBQA datasets. Results marked with ‘*’ are taken directly from the corresponding original papers. Bold numbers indicate the best performance, while underlined numbers denote the second-best (Hits@1).
Table 2: Ablation study of the graph-based soft prompting.
Table 3: Performance Comparison with Different LLMselect Settings.

Figure 2: Overview of the proposed framework and its running process. The upper part describes the method: GraSP is used to construct structure-aware soft prompts from the sub knowledge graph, which are first processed by a compact LLM to select relevant entities and relations, and then refined by a more powerful LLM with substantially more parameters to generate high-quality answers. The lower part demonstrates an example illustrating how our framework links Knews to Cyprus and subsequently treats Cyprus as the topic entity to derive the final answer.
Table 7: Performance comparison of different select LLMs.
